claims.claim: Israeli phone numbering uses +972 country code; mobile prefixes are 050-058 (10 digits), landline area codes are 02-04, 08, 09 (9 digits), and non-geographic / VoIP prefixes are 072-079.
Israel implemented Mobile Number Portability in 2007, so the leading prefix indicates the originally issuing carrier rather than the currently servicing carrier.
The 059 prefix matches the Israeli mobile shape but is assigned to Jawwal, a Palestinian mobile operator. The 056 prefix is similarly used by Wataniya in the Palestinian territories.
Israel's Ministry of Communications publishes official numbering-plan changes at gov.il under /departments/ministry_of_communications.
Israel's Ministry of Communications also publishes the Hebrew version of its homepage at gov.il/he/departments/ministry_of_communications.
Google's libphonenumber library provides the canonical E.164 validation and formatting rules used across major messaging SDKs and is hosted at github.com/google/libphonenumber.
The libphonenumber-js npm package is a JavaScript port of Google's libphonenumber commonly used in frontend phone-input components.
E.164 international format for Israeli mobile numbers strips the leading 0 from the domestic format (e.g., domestic 050-1234567 becomes international +972501234567).
Israeli emergency short codes: police 100, ambulance 101, fire 102.

Israeli star-service short codes follow the *XXXX shape with 4-6 digits after the star. *2421 is shown only as an illustrative example of that shape, with no claim about which carrier operates that specific code.
